title: Arisaema angustifoliolatum
intro: Arisaema angustifoliolatum, also known as the narrow-leaved cobra lily, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Arum family, characterized by its unique, hood-like spathe and poisonous berries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. Bioactive constituents of Arisaema angustifoliolatum include alkaloids, such as aristolochic acid and angustifoliolatin, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of dried rhizomes and roots, or as an extract in various commercial products.
